answers.field_label: Coronavirus Pandemic Extenuating Circumstances Description Line 1
answers.value: Since the COVID-19 pandemic, my household has struggled to fully recover financially because my partner’s hours as a line cook were significantly reduced and have never returned to pre-pandemic levels, which has left us consistently about one paycheck behind on several essential bills, including rent and utilities.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Coronavirus Pandemic Extenuating Circumstances Description Line 2
answers.value: During the 2020–2021 school year, I had to take multiple unpaid days to care for our two children when their school and daycare closed on short notice due to outbreaks, and although some assistance programs helped at the time, we relied on credit cards and a small high-interest personal loan to get through; we are still paying that off and the interest has made it difficult to catch up. My partner also contracted COVID twice (in late 2020 and again in 2022), which led to medical bills that were only partially covered by insurance. We deferred some payments back then, and now those deferred amounts have come due at the same time that our rent increased and our SNAP benefits were reduced. Using this solidarity fund loan, I intend to pay off the remaining balance on a high-interest utility arrears payment plan (heating and electric), bring our rent completely current so we are not at risk of a 5-day notice, and cover a remaining balance for my son’s asthma medication and inhaler refill, which have become more frequent and more expensive since he had COVID. Without this help, I am worried that one unexpected expense—like a car repair or an illness that forces me to miss work—will push us into a situation where we are choosing between paying rent and paying for groceries. This loan would stabilize us enough that we can manage our budget on my teaching salary and my partner’s variable income going forward.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Other Reason For Request Extenuating Circumstances Description Line 1
answers.value: Beyond the lingering pandemic-related impacts, our 2011 Honda CR-V needed an unexpected brake and tire replacement in November, which we had to put on a high-interest store credit card to ensure I could still get to school and my partner could get to evening shifts; that extra monthly payment has made it difficult to keep up with winter utility costs and has caused us to fall one month behind on our rent.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form

answers.field_label: Other Reason For Request Extenuating Circumstances Description Line 2
answers.value: Additionally, our younger child was recently diagnosed with mild developmental delays and started attending weekly occupational therapy sessions. While insurance covers a portion, we still pay a copay each visit plus transportation costs. I am also supporting my mother, who lives alone in Milwaukee and is on a fixed income; when her furnace went out in early December, I helped cover part of the repair bill using money we had set aside for January rent. I am not asking for help with discretionary expenses—this request is specifically to cover essential housing and utility costs, pay down the highest-interest portion of our debt that accumulated due to pandemic disruptions, and prevent us from sliding further behind. I have already contacted our utility company and landlord to arrange payment plans, cut back on non-essentials like streaming services, and picked up additional tutoring hours after school when possible. However, the gap caused by the compounding effects of the pandemic, medical costs, and emergency car repairs has left us in a situation where a one-time, no-interest loan would allow us to get current on critical bills, avoid late fees and shutoff notices, and return to a more stable, manageable monthly budget going forward.
answers.reasoning: Pre-filled value from ground truth form
